enrichment.short_description: Subscription billing and revenue management platform that automates recurring invoicing, payments, and revenue recognition for SaaS companies.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (up to $250K in cumulative billing, then 0.75% overage); Performance plan from $7,188/year
enrichment.headquarters: San Francisco, California, USA (originally founded in Chennai, India)

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Chargebee is a subscription billing and revenue management platform built for SaaS and subscription-based businesses that need to handle recurring payments, invoicing, and revenue operations at scale. Founded in 2011, the company grew out of Chennai, India and is now headquartered in San Francisco, California, with billing infrastructure that supports subscriptions across many countries and payment gateways.

At its core, Chargebee sits between a company's product and its payment processors, automating the mechanics of recurring revenue: creating and updating subscriptions, generating invoices, calculating taxes across jurisdictions, retrying failed payments, and feeding clean revenue data into accounting systems. It supports a range of pricing models — flat recurring fees, seat-based, usage/metered billing, and hybrid combinations — which has made it a common choice for SaaS companies whose pricing evolves as they grow.

Beyond core billing, Chargebee has expanded into adjacent revenue operations, including CPQ (quote-to-cash) tooling for sales-led deals, automated revenue recognition for accounting compliance, and a growth suite focused on subscriber acquisition and retention experiments. This makes it less a single tool and more a modular revenue platform that companies can adopt piece by piece.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Chargebee Is For
paragraphs: Chargebee is built for SaaS and subscription businesses — from early-stage startups signing their first paying customers to mid-market and enterprise companies running complex, multi-product subscription catalogs. Its free Starter tier, which covers the first $250,000 in cumulative billing, makes it accessible to small teams that want production-grade billing infrastructure without upfront cost.

It's a particularly strong fit for companies with pricing complexity: usage-based or metered billing, tiered plans, multi-currency sales, or hybrid models that combine subscriptions with one-time charges. Finance and RevOps teams that need dunning management to recover failed payments, and revenue recognition tooling to close the books accurately, also benefit from Chargebee's built-in modules rather than stitching together spreadsheets and custom scripts.

Larger organizations with multiple business entities, account hierarchies, or sales teams that need CPQ and quoting workflows can move into Chargebee's Enterprise tier, which is designed for that added structural complexity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Chargebee can be overkill for very simple businesses. A single-product company selling one flat-rate subscription plan with straightforward billing may find a lighter tool, or a payment processor's native billing feature, sufficient and cheaper.

Cost is a common friction point: the free tier's $250K cumulative billing cap and 0.75% overage fee mean that once a company scales past that threshold, or needs the Performance plan, ongoing costs can become significant and harder to predict, especially once add-on modules like Revenue Recognition or the Growth suite are layered on.

Teams without engineering or RevOps resources to configure and maintain the integration may also struggle. Getting the most out of Chargebee typically requires development work to wire up webhooks, custom fields, and integrations with a CRM or accounting system, rather than a purely no-code setup.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: Chargebee's core is its subscription billing engine, which supports flexible pricing models, automated invoicing, and multi-currency, multi-region tax handling, alongside integrations with numerous payment gateways so businesses aren't locked into a single processor.

Dunning management is one of its most cited strengths: Chargebee automatically retries failed payments on configurable schedules and sends recovery emails, aiming to reduce involuntary churn — a meaningful line item for subscription businesses. For finance teams, Chargebee's revenue recognition module automates compliant revenue reporting, while built-in analytics surface metrics like recurring revenue and churn.

On the growth side, the platform includes a self-serve customer portal, hosted checkout and pricing pages, and, in its Growth tier, tools for running pricing and offer experiments. Chargebee also connects to common business systems, including CRM and accounting platforms, so billing data can flow into wider company workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Chargebee Experience
paragraphs: Setting up Chargebee typically starts with modeling a company's product catalog and pricing plans inside its dashboard, then connecting one or more payment gateways and configuring tax settings for the regions being sold into. Most teams integrate Chargebee via its API and webhooks so that subscription state changes stay in sync with their own application.

The admin interface is dense, reflecting the breadth of what it configures, and new users generally need some ramp-up time to understand concepts like plans, add-ons, coupons, and entitlements. Chargebee offsets this with migration support and engineering consultation available on its paid tiers, plus extensive documentation.

Day to day, most customer-facing interaction happens through the self-serve customer portal and hosted checkout pages Chargebee generates, which can be styled to match a company's branding. For internal teams, the value tends to show up as reduced manual billing work, clearer revenue reporting, and fewer cancellations driven by failed payments once dunning is configured.

features.title: Recurring & Usage-Based Billing
features.description: Configure flat-rate, seat-based, tiered, usage/metered, and hybrid pricing models for subscription products.

features.title: Automated Invoicing & Tax Compliance
features.description: Generate invoices automatically and calculate sales tax/VAT across multiple regions and currencies.

features.title: Smart Dunning & Payment Recovery
features.description: Automatically retry failed payments on configurable schedules and send recovery emails to reduce involuntary churn.

features.title: Revenue Recognition
features.description: Automate compliant revenue recognition reporting for subscription and usage-based revenue streams.

features.title: Subscription & Entitlement Management
features.description: Manage plan changes, upgrades, downgrades, add-ons, coupons, and customer entitlements from a central dashboard.

features.title: Hosted Checkout & Customer Portal
features.description: Offer branded, self-serve checkout pages and a customer portal for managing subscriptions and payment details.

features.title: Multi-Gateway Payment Support
features.description: Connect to a wide range of payment gateways so billing isn't tied to a single processor.

features.title: CPQ / Quote-to-Cash
features.description: Create and manage sales quotes, including multi-product and multi-year deals, that flow directly into billing.

features.title: Analytics & Revenue Reporting
features.description: Track recurring revenue, churn, and other subscription metrics through built-in dashboards and reports.

features.title: Business System Integrations
features.description: Sync billing and subscription data with common CRM, accounting, and automation tools.

pros: Deep support for complex and evolving pricing models, including usage-based and hybrid billing
Built-in dunning management that helps recover failed payments and reduce churn
Broad ecosystem of payment gateway and business-tool integrations
Modular platform spanning billing, CPQ, revenue recognition, and growth tools that scales with the business
Free entry tier removes the upfront cost barrier for early-stage companies
cons: Costs can rise quickly past the free tier due to overage fees on billing volume
Advanced modules and Enterprise pricing require contacting sales, making total costs hard to predict upfront
Admin interface and configuration have a learning curve for non-technical teams
Getting full value typically requires developer time to integrate the API and webhooks
Stacking multiple product lines (Billing, CPQ, Revenue Recognition, Growth) adds separate pricing and complexity

faqs.question: What is Chargebee used for?
faqs.answer: Chargebee is a subscription billing and revenue management platform used to automate recurring invoicing, payment collection, dunning, and revenue recognition for subscription-based businesses.

faqs.question: Does Chargebee have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chargebee's Starter plan is free for the first $250,000 in cumulative billing, after which a 0.75% overage fee applies.

faqs.question: Does Chargebee support usage-based billing?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chargebee supports usage-based and metered billing alongside flat recurring and seat-based pricing models.

faqs.question: What payment gateways does Chargebee work with?
faqs.answer: Chargebee integrates with a large number of payment gateways, including major providers, so businesses can process payments without being locked into a single processor.

faqs.question: Does Chargebee handle revenue recognition?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chargebee offers a dedicated revenue recognition module that automates compliant revenue reporting for subscription and usage-based revenue.

use_cases.title: Startup Subscription Billing
use_cases.description: Launch and manage recurring billing for a new SaaS product without building custom payment infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Early-stage SaaS founders and small teams

use_cases.title: Usage-Based Billing for Platforms
use_cases.description: Meter and bill customers based on consumption for API, infrastructure, or platform products.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Revenue Operations
use_cases.description: Manage multi-entity billing, account hierarchies, and revenue recognition across a large subscriber base.
use_cases.audience: Finance and RevOps teams at larger organizations

use_cases.title: Sales-Led Quote-to-Cash
use_cases.description: Create sales quotes for complex deals and convert them directly into active subscriptions and invoices.
use_cases.audience: B2B sales teams closing negotiated contracts

pricing_plans.price: Free (up to $250K cumulative billing, then 0.75% overage)
